creator: j.david.lists@gmail.com
text:
I have also encountered this issue.  It appears to be an incompatibility between pango and Python 3.7 due to PEP 479 ( https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0479/ ).

This will most likely have to be fixed upstream by the Pango/Gnome developers, not by the FreeBSD Gnome team.

The workaround is to step back to Python 3.6 until the issue is resolved.
